title: Stat 558: Project 1
text:
The goal is to write functions manipulating and processing data sets originating from a census application programming interface (API). Generic functions will be created to automatically summarize and plot some returned data. We load the necessary libraries in this first code section.

library(tidyverse)

── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
✔ dplyr     1.2.1     ✔ readr     2.2.0
✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
✔ ggplot2   4.0.3     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
✔ purrr     1.2.2     
── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors

library(dplyr)
library(httr)
library(jsonlite)


Attaching package: 'jsonlite'

The following object is masked from 'package:purrr':

    flatten

library(janitor)


Attaching package: 'janitor'

The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':

    chisq.test, fisher.test




We will be using the Public Use Microdata Sample (PUMS) Census API. Information about variables from selected years may be found here by changing the year section (here, \(2024\)).
